codeReview.summary: Implementation fully covers the feature requirements: parses CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files for operator-provided patterns with 0.95 base confidence (higher than agent-learned patterns at 0.4-0.7), includes mtime-based change detection for sync, and integrates with existing pattern_learning conventions system. 19 tests cover parser, service, patterns, conventions, and config.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The AGENTS.md documents coding conventions but does not specifically mention that CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files are parsed for pattern extraction. This is an implicit pattern that workers discovered by reading the codebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: AGENTS.md at /Users/ludwigjonsson/.factory/missions/67c2b690-f32a-4538-8ff8-3eb2790dc44e/AGENTS.md focuses on mission boundaries, coding conventions, and commands, but does not document that operator feedback files are parsed for pattern learning.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill (.factory/skills/rust/SKILL.md) does not mention operator feedback pattern parsing. Worker followed correct Rust idioms but discovered the feature implementation pattern by reading the codebase.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Worker used async_trait, thiserror, proper error handling with Result<T, SwellError>, and tokio::sync::RwLock - all consistent with codebase conventions. The skill file provides general Rust guidance but doesn't specifically cover this pattern extraction domain.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker spent time understanding the existing pattern_learning conventions system and ConventionSource enum to properly integrate the new operator feedback source. This domain knowledge about confidence hierarchy (FromOperatorFeedback > FromAntiPattern > FromSuccessfulTask) was not explicitly documented.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: pattern_learning.rs shows confidence hierarchy: anti-patterns capped at 0.95, successful tasks use 0.4-0.7. The operator_feedback.rs correctly uses 0.95 base confidence which is higher than learned patterns. Worker integrated with patterns_to_conventions() method.

summary: Feature passes review. Implementation correctly parses CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md files with 0.95 base confidence (higher than agent-learned patterns at 0.4-0.7), includes mtime-based change detection for sync, and integrates with existing pattern_learning conventions. All 19 tests pass. No blocking issues found. Minor observation: confidence hierarchy not explicitly documented in AGENTS.md or skill files.
